general.operation_type: a small beef cattle operation with hay feeding, pasture use, and seasonal barnyard concentration
general.animals: 24 beef cows, 8 replacement heifers, and seasonal calves
general.housing_areas: old bank barn, open barnyard south of the barn, and seasonal pasture access
general.manure_system: solid manure is scraped from the barnyard and stacked before field application

farmstead.description: main production area includes the old barn, a south barnyard, hay feeding area, temporary manure stacking area, and farm driveway
farmstead.animal_housing: cattle use the old barn during winter feeding and have access to the barnyard during wet and cold periods
farmstead.barnyards: the primary barnyard is south of the barn and receives repeated cattle and tractor traffic
farmstead.heavy_use_areas: traffic is concentrated along the feed lane and barn entrance
farmstead.manure_storage: solid manure is stacked on a compacted area north of the barn until conditions allow spreading
farmstead.feed_storage: dry hay is stored inside and baleage is staged near the barnyard during winter feeding
farmstead.drainage_patterns: runoff generally moves east from the barnyard toward a grassed swale and road ditch
farmstead.sensitive_features: a road ditch is east of the farmstead and a water well is located near the house
farmstead.clean_water_contamination: roof runoff from the south barn roof crosses the barnyard before reaching the swale
farmstead.contaminant_exit: manure and sediment could leave the barnyard through the low area along the east driveway edge
farmstead.current_management: the operator scrapes manure when conditions allow and adds stone to the driveway as needed
farmstead.observed_concerns: clean water becomes contaminated when it crosses the barnyard, and manure-laden runoff could leave the production area during rainfall or snowmelt

barnyards.location_size: approximately 0.25 acre south of the old barn
barnyards.animals_using: 24 beef cows and replacement heifers use the area during winter feeding and wet conditions
barnyards.seasonal_use: most heavily from late fall through early spring
barnyards.surface_condition: mixed gravel and soil with soft areas near the feed lane
barnyards.vegetative_cover: limited cover in the main traffic area
barnyards.mud: mud is present near the barn entrance after rainfall and thaw events
barnyards.manure_accumulation: manure accumulates along the scraping lane and around the feed area
barnyards.runoff_direction: runoff flows east toward the driveway edge and grassed swale
barnyards.nearby_resources: road ditch east of the barnyard
barnyards.connections: the barnyard connects the old barn, feed lane, driveway, and pasture gate
barnyards.management: area is scraped when equipment access is possible
barnyards.concerns: persistent livestock traffic, exposed soil, manure accumulation, and drainage toward the ditch create a contaminated runoff concern

roof_runoff_clean_water.roof_areas: south roof of the old barn contributes runoff directly to the barnyard
roof_runoff_clean_water.gutters: gutters are incomplete and do not fully collect roof runoff
roof_runoff_clean_water.separation: clean roof water is not fully separated from manure and traffic areas
roof_runoff_clean_water.underground_outlets: no underground outlet is currently present
roof_runoff_clean_water.discharge: clean water would need a stable discharge away from the barnyard and manure stack
roof_runoff_clean_water.concerns: clean water is converted to contaminated runoff when it crosses manure and exposed barnyard surfaces

pasture.season: spring through fall as forage conditions allow
pasture.condition: pasture cover is generally stable away from the barnyard gate
pasture.water_sources: portable trough supplied from the farmstead
pasture.shade: limited natural shade along field edges
pasture.concentrated_flow: flow concentrates near the gate during wet weather
pasture.bare_soil: bare soil is present near the pasture gate and water trough
pasture.sacrifice_lot: barnyard functions as the wet-weather sacrifice area
pasture.stream_access: no direct stream access was documented
pasture.management: operator rotates access when forage height allows
pasture.concerns: concentrated traffic near the gate and water trough can expose soil and move sediment toward the farmstead drainage pattern
